reasoning: The resolution window spans from March 29, 2026 (closing price on March 27, 2026 due to weekend) to April 5, 2026. The stock market is closed on Friday, April 3, 2026, for Good Friday. Therefore, the closing price to be used for the April 5 resolution will be the closing price on Thursday, April 2, 2026. This creates a 4-trading-day window (Monday, March 30, through Thursday, April 2). Crucially, the ex-dividend date for Republic Services' upcoming $0.625 dividend is exactly on Thursday, April 2. This means on the morning of April 2, the stock price will automatically be adjusted downwards by $0.625 (a ~0.29% drop). Over only 4 trading days, the usual market upward drift and RSG's historical positive momentum are not strong enough to completely offset this automatic headwind in more than half of the scenarios. While the time-series tool estimates 55% chance based strictly on a continuous linear trend over 60 days, introducing this highly specific discrete ex-dividend drag on the exact boundary day tips the overall probability down to roughly 46%.
